questions.question: Why is the chunking choice an upper bound on retrieval quality and not just a hyperparameter?
options: Because the LLM never reads chunks
Because the embedding model is fixed at training time
Because no embedding, reranker, or LLM downstream can recover an answer span that was cut into incoherent pieces
Because BM25 has no role in modern RAG
questions.explanation: If the chunk that holds the answer span is incoherent the embedding moves to the wrong cluster and downstream components cannot repair the damage.

questions.question: Which strategy is the strongest single-strategy default when you have no prior knowledge of the corpus?
options: Fixed-window
Sentence packer
Recursive split
Structural markdown
questions.explanation: Recursive split adapts per region by trying the strongest separator first and falling back, so it survives inconsistent structure.

questions.question: What does recall@k measure in this lesson?
options: Whether the top-k chunks contain the literal query string
Whether any of the top-k chunks overlaps the gold answer span
The cosine similarity between the query and the top-k chunks
The number of unique documents in the top-k
questions.explanation: Each query carries gold offsets; recall@k is 1 if any top-k chunk overlaps the gold span, else 0; averaged across queries.

questions.question: Why does fixed-window chunking fail badly on code or YAML corpora?
options: It loses Unicode characters
It cuts identifiers and key-value pairs in half, producing chunks that embed to noise
It produces chunks larger than the embedding model's context window
It cannot be parallelized
questions.explanation: Character-level cuts inside a symbol destroy the meaning the embedding model is supposed to capture.

questions.question: What is the centroid drift termination criterion in semantic chunking?
options: Cosine similarity between the running mean vector and the next sentence drops below a threshold
Character count exceeds the max
Sentence count exceeds a hardcoded limit
The chunker has consumed the entire embedding budget
questions.explanation: Semantic chunking cuts when the running centroid no longer matches the next sentence by a configured similarity threshold.

questions.question: What stale-embedding failure mode does semantic chunking introduce that the other four strategies do not?
options: Chunks too small for the embedding context
Chunk boundaries change whenever you change the chunk-model, requiring a coordinated rebuild
Chunks are no longer indexable by BM25
Chunks lose their start and end offsets
questions.explanation: Semantic clustering depends on an embedding model; swapping the model changes the boundaries, so the chunk-model must be pinned separately or rebuilt together with the retrieval model.
